author: claude
content:
Draft PR opened: https://github.com/openwpm/OpenWPM/pull/1207 (branch harden/legacy-error-stacks, based on master).

What landed:
- Back-ported stealth's stack-cleaning predicate to the legacy page-world instrument. Added two INLINE helpers inside getInstrumentJS in Extension/src/lib/js-instruments.ts: cleanErrorStack(stack) (drops every stack line containing moz-extension://) and rethrowWithCleanStack(err) (overwrites the caught error's own .stack with the cleaned value and re-throws the SAME object, preserving error type/identity).
- Wrapped the three page-observable call sites in try/catch -> rethrowWithCleanStack: instrumentFunction's func.apply(this, arguments), and the instrumented getter's/setter's original{Getter,Setter}.call(this).
- Page-world adaptation: legacy already runs in the page world (no Xray boundary), so no wrappedJSObject reconstruction is needed -- we just overwrite the error's own .stack. Thrown primitives (no .stack) re-throw unchanged.
- Helpers are inline so they survive being stringified into the page <script> via ${getInstrumentJS} (no es6 imports). Confirmed the helpers are present in the compiled content.js bundle.
- Regression test: test/test_js_instrument_error_stack.py + test/test_pages/js_instrument/instrument_error_stack.html. Instruments window.atob, forces it to throw (invalid base64 -> InvalidCharacterError), catches in page code, asserts the page-observable e.stack contains NO moz-extension:// frame.

Verification: extension build green, npm run lint green, python pre-commit (isort/black/mypy) green. BLOCKER: the browser regression test could not be run to completion locally -- the OpenWPM browser-spawn path fails for ALL browser tests in this env, including unmodified pre-existing ones (spawn failure before profile creation; root cause is fork-based multiprocess spawn under Python 3.14.6, not this change -- a direct headless FF152+geckodriver launch works). Left as DRAFT so CI validates the browser test.
